revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)]][[ja:八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Nagoya, Tōkai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Hachimanyama Kofun | native_name =八幡山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hachimanyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Hachimanyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|09|14|N|136|55|26|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Shōwa-ku, Nagoya|Shōwa-ku, Nagoya]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Hachimanyama Kofun'''|八幡山古墳}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] in the Yamawaki neighborhood of {{ill|Shōwa-ku|en|Shōwa-ku|qid=Q1155273|lt=Shōwa Ward|1=Shōwa-ku}} in the city of {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|qid=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Dating from the mid-fifth century, it is the largest circular ''kofun'' in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1931.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/detail|title=八幡山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Hachimanyama Kofun is a large circular ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumulus, with a diameter of 82 meters and height of 10 meters. It was once surrounded by a 10-meter wide moat, which is now filled in. It is one of the largest circular Kofuns in the Tōkai region, and built in the middleof the 5th century AD. The name "Hachimanyama" came from a small {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, the Hachiman Jinja, which was once located on its summit. This ''kofun'' is the largest and only survivor of a group of tumuli which once existed in the area. It was incorporated into Tsurumai Park in 1919, at which time the tumulus was recorded as being covered with a thick grove of pine trees. During {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the site was seized by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|qid=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}}, and the summit was flattened for use as the location of an anti-aircraft battery. After the war, the mound was remodeled by the city of Nagoya, and trees were replanted; however, no [[archaeological excavation]] was made and the site was fenced off with public access prohibited. Per pre-war records, numerous ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' had been recovered from the site, including some in the shape of human figures, but these were lost in the war and their present whereabouts (or even if they still survive) is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The ''kofun'' is located about 8 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} {{ill|Tsurumai Station|en|Tsurumai Station|qid=Q855581|lt=Tsurumai Station|1=Tsurumai Station}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|qid=Q9652090|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== [[Category:Hachimanyama kofun (Aichi)}} *[http://itot.jp/23107/20 Nagoya Showa-ku Area Guiide] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.aichitabi.com/nagoya/kofun.html Aichi Tourist Information Guide] {{in lang|ja}}
